old_claim.note: Lateen Sail & Caravel provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Trade Leagues and Lateen Sail & Caravel; Medieval trade leagues such as the Hanseatic League developed around merchant and city networks, not Iberian caravels.

source_shape.source_locator: Article anchors the Hanseatic League to medieval northern European trade networks and does not make Iberian caravels a prerequisite for trade leagues.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Medieval trade leagues such as the Hanseatic League developed around merchant and city networks, not Iberian caravels.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Trade Leagues claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Lateen Sail & Caravel as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Trade Leagues treated Lateen Sail & Caravel as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Trade Leagues no longer directly depends on Lateen Sail & Caravel; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: trade_leagues must not directly depend on lateen_sail_caravel unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows trade_leagues is intentionally scoped only to cases that require lateen_sail_caravel.
The graph splits trade_leagues and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on lateen_sail_caravel.
A stronger source demonstrates that lateen_sail_caravel is chronologically and causally necessary for trade_leagues.
short_reason: Medieval trade leagues such as the Hanseatic League developed around merchant and city networks, not Iberian caravels.
